key_insight: Fovea-centered spatial normalization can reduce geometric domain shift and improve cross-dataset retinal layer segmentation in OCT.
clinical_relevance: More consistent OCT layer segmentation could support reliable retinal biomarkers for neurodegenerative disease research and downstream quantitative analysis.
method: Aligned OCT volumes to a common anatomical reference centered on the fovea
Benchmarked multiple state-of-the-art deep learning segmentation models
Evaluated with overlap, topology-aware, and thickness-based metrics across B-scan, A-scan, and en-face views
evidence_signal: The study reports a comprehensive evaluation using conventional segmentation metrics plus topology-violation and thickness-based measures, including settings without ground-truth annotations.
watch_item: Normalization may improve robustness, but its performance depends on accurate fovea alignment and may not fully address noise, shadowing, or other non-geometric domain shifts.
paper_title: Spatial Normalization for Cross-Domain Retinal Layer Segmentation in Optical Coherence Tomography
